findings.excerpt: - Use tabs for indentation in Makefiles
findings.message: Tabs (line 8) vs spaces (line 7) for indentation.
findings.recommendation: Pick one convention, delete the other, and let a linter or lockfile enforce it where possible.

findings.excerpt: Run `npm install` then `yarn build` to get started.
findings.message: Mixed package managers in commands: npm, yarn.
findings.recommendation: Pick one convention, delete the other, and let a linter or lockfile enforce it where possible.

findings.message: Token-shaped string: API key (sk-...).
findings.recommendation: Remove the credential, rotate it, and reference an env var name instead.

findings.message: Formatter-enforceable style rule stated as prose.
findings.recommendation: Delete the rule and point to the linter instead, e.g. "run `eslint --fix`; the linter config is the source of truth."

findings.message: Vague phrase "write clean code" — nothing testable for the agent.
findings.recommendation: Replace with a concrete, verifiable instruction (exact command, path, or example) or delete the line.

findings.message: Directory-tree block of 9 lines.
findings.recommendation: Delete the tree. Keep at most one line per genuinely non-obvious location ("API handlers live in src/api/handlers/").

findings.excerpt: - Write tests for all new features because quality matters here
findings.message: Duplicates line 41.
findings.recommendation: Keep one occurrence, delete the rest.

findings.message: Cluster of doc links with no instruction to read them.
findings.ruleName: Doc pointers without a read instruction
findings.recommendation: Prefix the list with an explicit imperative, e.g. "IMPORTANT: read the relevant doc below before starting work."

findings.message: Zero-exception rule expressed as advisory prose.
findings.recommendation: Keep the line if you accept ~advisory compliance; for zero-exception rules add a hook, lint rule, or CI check.

findings.message: Zero-exception rule expressed as advisory prose.
findings.recommendation: Keep the line if you accept ~advisory compliance; for zero-exception rules add a hook, lint rule, or CI check.

findings.excerpt: - You must never use console.log in production code
findings.message: Zero-exception rule expressed as advisory prose.
findings.recommendation: Keep the line if you accept ~advisory compliance; for zero-exception rules add a hook, lint rule, or CI check.

findings.excerpt: IMPORTANT: quality is CRITICAL. YOU MUST take this seriously.
findings.message: 6 emphasis markers (IMPORTANT/YOU MUST/CRITICAL) — past ~5 they cancel out.
findings.recommendation: Keep emphasis on the 1-3 rules that genuinely matter most; demote the rest to plain statements.

agentNotes.note: Injects CLAUDE.md as an advisory user message (no compliance guarantee); official size target is under 200 lines. Zero-exception rules belong in hooks.
Reads AGENTS.md natively (nearest file wins, 32 KiB cap — this file is 1.2 KiB). Supports AGENTS.override.md for local tweaks.
Reads AGENTS.md natively. Path-scoped rules need .cursor/rules/*.mdc — plain .md files there are silently ignored.
Reads AGENTS.md natively (coding agent, since Aug 2025) alongside .github/copilot-instructions.md.
